channel.title: Curry on Memo
channel.description: Recent content in Curry on Memo

item.title: Install Curry
item.description: Install Curry # Curry is a language that has features of both functional and logic programming languages. PAKCS is an interpreter that can execute programs written in Curry. PAKCS converts Curry code into Prolog and executes it. Install # Arch paru -S pakcs Ubuntu sudo apt-add-repository ppa:swi-prolog/stable sudo apt update sudo apt install swi-prolog sudo apt install pakcs download site Basic Usage # Sample program: -- family.curry data Person = Alice | Bob | Charlie | Dave parent Alice = True parent Bob = True child Charlie = True child Dave = True parent_child Alice Charlie = True parent_child Alice Dave = True parent_child Bob Charlie = True parent_child Bob Dave = True sibling x y = parent_child z x & parent_child z y where z free Enter interactive shell with pakcs: $ pakcs which: no rlwrap in (/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/bin/site_perl:/usr/bin/vendor_perl:/usr/bin/core_perl) >>> /home/user/.
